verses.latin_vulgate: Et præteriens Jesus vidit hominem cæcum a nativitate:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ παράγων εἶδεν ἄνθρωπον τυφλὸν ἐκ γενετῆς
verses.king_james: And as *Jesus* passed by, he saw a man which was blind from *his* birth.

verses.latin_vulgate: et interrogaverunt eum discipuli ejus: Rabbi, quis peccavit, hic, aut parentes ejus, ut cæcus nasceretur?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἠρώτησαν αὐτὸν οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ λέγοντες ῥαββί τίς ἥμαρτεν οὗτος ἢ οἱ γονεῖς αὐτοῦ ἵνα τυφλὸς γεννηθῇ
verses.king_james: And his disciples asked him, saying, Master, who did sin, this man, or his parents, that he was born blind?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus: Neque hic peccavit, neque parentes ejus: sed ut manifestentur opera Dei in illo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἰησοῦς οὔτε οὗτος ἥμαρτεν οὔτε οἱ γονεῖς αὐτοῦ ἀλλ᾽ ἵνα φανερωθῇ τὰ ἔργα τοῦ θεοῦ ἐν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: Jesus answered, Neither hath this man sinned, nor his parents: but that the works of God should be made manifest in him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Me oportet operari opera ejus qui misit me, donec dies est: venit nox, quando nemo potest operari:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐμὲ δεῖ ἐργάζεσθαι τὰ ἔργα τοῦ πέμψαντός με ἕως ἡμέρα ἐστίν ἔρχεται νὺξ ὅτε οὐδεὶς δύναται ἐργάζεσθαι
verses.king_james: I must work the works of him that sent me, while it is day: the night cometh, when no man can work.

verses.latin_vulgate: quamdiu sum in mundo, lux sum mundi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν ἐν τῷ κόσμῳ ὦ φῶς εἰμι τοῦ κόσμου
verses.king_james: As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc cum dixisset, exspuit in terram, et fecit lutum ex sputo, et linivit lutum super oculos ejus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα εἰπὼν ἔπτυσεν χαμαὶ καὶ ἐποίησεν πηλὸν ἐκ τοῦ πτύσματος καὶ ἐπέχρισεν τὸν πηλὸν ἐπὶ τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς τοῦ τυφλοῦ,
verses.king_james: When he had thus spoken, he spat on the ground, and made clay of the spittle, and he anointed the eyes of the blind man with the clay,

verses.latin_vulgate: et dixit ei: Vade, lava in natatoria Siloë (quod interpretatur Missus). Abiit ergo, et lavit, et venit videns.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ὕπαγε νίψαι εἰς τὴν κολυμβήθραν τοῦ σιλωάμ ὃ ἑρμηνεύεται ἀπεσταλμένος ἀπῆλθεν οὖν καὶ ἐνίψατο καὶ ἦλθεν βλέπων
verses.king_james: And said unto him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam, (which is by interpretation, Sent.) He went his way therefore, and washed, and came seeing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque vicini, et qui viderant eum prius quia mendicus erat, dicebant: Nonne hic est qui sedebat, et mendicabat? Alii dicebant: Quia hic est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ οὖν γείτονες καὶ οἱ θεωροῦντες αὐτὸν τὸ πρότερον ὅτι τυφλὸς ἦν ἔλεγον οὐχ οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ καθήμενος καὶ προσαιτῶν
verses.king_james: The neighbours therefore, and they which before had seen him that he was blind, said, Is not this he that sat and begged?

verses.latin_vulgate: Alii autem: Nequaquam, sed similis est ei. Ille vero dicebat: Quia ego sum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄλλοι ἔλεγον ὅτι οὗτός ἐστιν ἄλλοι δὲ, ὅτι ὅμοιος αὐτῷ ἐστιν ἐκεῖνος ἔλεγεν ὅτι ἐγώ εἰμι
verses.king_james: Some said, This is he: others *said*, He is like him: *but* he said, I am *he*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebant ergo ei: Quomodo aperti sunt tibi oculi?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλεγον οὖν αὐτῷ πῶς ἀνεῴχθησάν σου οἱ ὀφθαλμοί
verses.king_james: Therefore said they unto him, How were thine eyes opened?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit: Ille homo qui dicitur Jesus, lutum fecit: et unxit oculos meos, et dixit mihi: Vade ad natatoria Siloë, et lava. Et abii, et lavi, et video.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ἐκεῖνος καὶ εἶπέν ἄνθρωπος λεγόμενος ἰησοῦς πηλὸν ἐποίησεν καὶ ἐπέχρισέν μου τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς καὶ εἶπεν, μοι ὕπαγε εἰς τὴν κολυμβήθραν τοῦ σιλωὰμ καὶ νίψαι ἀπελθὼν δὲ καὶ νιψάμενος ἀνέβλεψα
verses.king_james: He answered and said, A man that is called Jesus made clay, and anointed mine eyes, and said unto me, Go to the pool of Siloam, and wash: and I went and washed, and I received sight.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixerunt ei: Ubi est ille? Ait: Nescio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπον οὖν αὐτῷ ποῦ ἐστιν ἐκεῖνος λέγει οὐκ οἶδα
verses.king_james: Then said they unto him, Where is he? He said, I know not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adducunt eum ad pharisæos, qui cæcus fuerat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄγουσιν αὐτὸν πρὸς τοὺς φαρισαίους τόν ποτε τυφλόν
verses.king_james: They brought to the Pharisees him that aforetime was blind.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem sabbatum quando lutum fecit Jesus, et aperuit oculos ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ σάββατον ὅτε τὸν πηλὸν ἐποίησεν ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ ἀνέῳξεν αὐτοῦ τοὺς ὀφθαλμούς
verses.king_james: And it was the sabbath day when Jesus made the clay, and opened his eyes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Iterum ergo interrogabant eum pharisæi quomodo vidisset. Ille autem dixit eis: Lutum mihi posuit super oculos, et lavi, et video.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάλιν οὖν ἠρώτων αὐτὸν καὶ οἱ φαρισαῖοι πῶς ἀνέβλεψεν ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς πηλὸν ἐπέθηκέν ἐπὶ τοὺς ὀφθαλμούς μου καὶ ἐνιψάμην καὶ βλέπω
verses.king_james: Then again the Pharisees also asked him how he had received his sight. He said unto them, He put clay upon mine eyes, and I washed, and do see.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebant ergo ex pharisæis quidam: Non est hic homo a Deo, qui sabbatum non custodit. Alii autem dicebant: Quomodo potest homo peccator hæc signa facere? Et schisma erat inter eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλεγον οὖν ἐκ τῶν φαρισαίων τινές οὗτος ὁ ἄνθρωπος οὐκ ἔστιν παρὰ τοῦ θεοῦ ὅτι τὸ σάββατον οὐ τηρεῖ ἄλλοι ἔλεγον πῶς δύναται ἄνθρωπος ἁμαρτωλὸς τοιαῦτα σημεῖα ποιεῖν καὶ σχίσμα ἦν ἐν αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: Therefore said some of the Pharisees, This man is not of God, because he keepeth not the sabbath day. Others said, How can a man that is a sinner do such miracles? And there was a division among them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicunt ergo cæco iterum: Tu quid dicis de illo qui aperuit oculos tuos? Ille autem dixit: Quia propheta est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγουσιν τῷ τυφλῷ πάλιν σὺ τί λέγεις περὶ αὐτοῦ ὅτι ἤνοιξεν σου τοὺς ὀφθαλμούς ὁ δὲ εἶπεν ὅτι προφήτης ἐστίν
verses.king_james: They say unto the blind man again, What sayest thou of him, that he hath opened thine eyes? He said, He is a prophet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non crediderunt ergo Judæi de illo, quia cæcus fuisset et vidisset, donec vocaverunt parentes ejus, qui viderat:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἐπίστευσαν οὖν οἱ ἰουδαῖοι περὶ αὐτοῦ ὅτι τυφλὸς ἦν καὶ ἀνέβλεψεν ἕως ὅτου ἐφώνησαν τοὺς γονεῖς αὐτοῦ τοῦ ἀναβλέψαντος
verses.king_james: But the Jews did not believe concerning him, that he had been blind, and received his sight, until they called the parents of him that had received his sight.

verses.latin_vulgate: et interrogaverunt eos, dicentes: Hic est filius vester, quem vos dicitis quia cæcus natus est? quomodo ergo nunc videt?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἠρώτησαν αὐτοὺς λέγοντες οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ υἱὸς ὑμῶν ὃν ὑμεῖς λέγετε ὅτι τυφλὸς ἐγεννήθη πῶς οὖν ἄρτι βλέπει
verses.king_james: And they asked them, saying, Is this your son, who ye say was born blind? how then doth he now see?

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderunt eis parentes ejus, et dixerunt: Scimus quia hic est filius noster, et quia cæcus natus est:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθησαν αὐτοῖς οἱ γονεῖς αὐτοῦ καὶ εἶπον, οἴδαμεν ὅτι οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ υἱὸς ἡμῶν καὶ ὅτι τυφλὸς ἐγεννήθη
verses.king_james: His parents answered them and said, We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind:

verses.latin_vulgate: quomodo autem nunc videat, nescimus: aut quis ejus aperuit oculos, nos nescimus; ipsum interrogate: ætatem habet, ipse de se loquatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πῶς δὲ νῦν βλέπει οὐκ οἴδαμεν ἢ τίς ἤνοιξεν αὐτοῦ τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς ἡμεῖς οὐκ οἴδαμεν αὐτὸς ἡλικίαν ἔχει αὐτὸν ἐρωτήσατε αὐτὸς περὶ αὐτοῦ λαλήσει
verses.king_james: But by what means he now seeth, we know not; or who hath opened his eyes, we know not: he is of age; ask him: he shall speak for himself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dixerunt parentes ejus, quoniam timebant Judæos: jam enim conspiraverunt Judæi, ut si quis eum confiteretur esse Christum, extra synagogam fieret.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα εἶπον οἱ γονεῖς αὐτοῦ ὅτι ἐφοβοῦντο τοὺς ἰουδαίους ἤδη γὰρ συνετέθειντο οἱ ἰουδαῖοι ἵνα ἐάν τις αὐτὸν ὁμολογήσῃ χριστόν ἀποσυνάγωγος γένηται
verses.king_james: These *words* spake his parents, because they feared the Jews: for the Jews had agreed already, that if any man did confess that he was Christ, he should be put out of the synagogue.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea parentes ejus dixerunt: Quia ætatem habet, ipsum interrogate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τοῦτο οἱ γονεῖς αὐτοῦ εἶπον, ὅτι ἡλικίαν ἔχει αὐτὸν ἐρωτήσατε
verses.king_james: Therefore said his parents, He is of age; ask him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vocaverunt ergo rursum hominem qui fuerat cæcus, et dixerunt ei: Da gloriam Deo: nos scimus quia hic homo peccator est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐφώνησαν οὖν ἐκ δευτέρου τὸν ἄνθρωπον ὃς ἦν τυφλὸς καὶ εἶπον αὐτῷ δὸς δόξαν τῷ θεῷ ἡμεῖς οἴδαμεν ὅτι ὁ ἄνθρωπος οὗτος ἁμαρτωλός ἐστιν
verses.king_james: Then again called they the man that was blind, and said unto him, Give God the praise: we know that this man is a sinner.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ergo eis ille: Si peccator est, nescio; unum scio, quia cæcus cum essem, modo video.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη οὖν ἐκεῖνος καὶ εἶπεν, εἰ ἁμαρτωλός ἐστιν οὐκ οἶδα ἓν οἶδα ὅτι τυφλὸς ὢν ἄρτι βλέπω
verses.king_james: He answered and said, Whether he be a sinner *or no*, I know not: one thing I know, that, whereas I was blind, now I see.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixerunt ergo illi: Quid fecit tibi? quomodo aperuit tibi oculos?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπον δὲ αὐτῷ πάλιν, τί ἐποίησέν σοι πῶς ἤνοιξέν σου τοὺς ὀφθαλμούς
verses.king_james: Then said they to him again, What did he to thee? how opened he thine eyes?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit eis: Dixi vobis jam, et audistis: quod iterum vultis audire? numquid et vos vultis discipuli ejus fieri?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη αὐτοῖς εἶπον ὑμῖν ἤδη καὶ οὐκ ἠκούσατε τί πάλιν θέλετε ἀκούειν μὴ καὶ ὑμεῖς θέλετε αὐτοῦ μαθηταὶ γενέσθαι
verses.king_james: He answered them, I have told you already, and ye did not hear: wherefore would ye hear *it* again? will ye also be his disciples?

verses.latin_vulgate: Maledixerunt ergo ei, et dixerunt: Tu discipulus illius sis: nos autem Moysi discipuli sumus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλοιδόρησαν οὖν αὐτὸν καὶ εἶπον σὺ εἶ μαθητὴς ἐκείνου ἡμεῖς δὲ τοῦ μωσέως ἐσμὲν μαθηταί
verses.king_james: Then they reviled him, and said, Thou art his disciple; but we are Moses' disciples.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nos scimus quia Moysi locutus est Deus; hunc autem nescimus unde sit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡμεῖς οἴδαμεν ὅτι μωσῃ λελάληκεν ὁ θεός τοῦτον δὲ οὐκ οἴδαμεν πόθεν ἐστίν
verses.king_james: We know that God spake unto Moses: *as for* this *fellow*, we know not from whence he is.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit ille homo, et dixit eis: In hoc enim mirabile est quia vos nescitis unde sit, et aperuit meos oculos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἄνθρωπος καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ἐν γὰρ τούτῳ θαυμαστόν ἐστιν ὅτι ὑμεῖς οὐκ οἴδατε πόθεν ἐστίν καὶ ἀνέῳξεν μου τοὺς ὀφθαλμούς
verses.king_james: The man answered and said unto them, Why herein is a marvellous thing, that ye know not from whence he is, and *yet* he hath opened mine eyes.

verses.latin_vulgate: scimus autem quia peccatores Deus non audit: sed si quis Dei cultor est, et voluntatem ejus facit, hunc exaudit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἴδαμεν δὲ ὅτι ἁμαρτωλῶν ὁ θεὸς οὐκ ἀκούει ἀλλ᾽ ἐάν τις θεοσεβὴς ᾖ καὶ τὸ θέλημα αὐτοῦ ποιῇ τούτου ἀκούει
verses.king_james: Now we know that God heareth not sinners: but if any man be a worshipper of God, and doeth his will, him he heareth.

verses.latin_vulgate: A sæculo non est auditum quia quis aperuit oculos cæci nati.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκ τοῦ αἰῶνος οὐκ ἠκούσθη ὅτι ἤνοιξεν τις ὀφθαλμοὺς τυφλοῦ γεγεννημένου
verses.king_james: Since the world began was it not heard that any man opened the eyes of one that was born blind.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nisi esset hic a Deo, non poterat facere quidquam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ μὴ ἦν οὗτος παρὰ θεοῦ οὐκ ἠδύνατο ποιεῖν οὐδέν
verses.king_james: If this man were not of God, he could do nothing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderunt, et dixerunt ei: In peccatis natus es totus, et tu doces nos? Et ejecerunt eum foras.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθησαν καὶ εἶπον αὐτῷ ἐν ἁμαρτίαις σὺ ἐγεννήθης ὅλος καὶ σὺ διδάσκεις ἡμᾶς καὶ ἐξέβαλον αὐτὸν ἔξω
verses.king_james: They answered and said unto him, Thou wast altogether born in sins, and dost thou teach us? And they cast him out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audivit Jesus quia ejecerunt eum foras: et cum invenisset eum, dixit ei: Tu credis in Filium Dei?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἤκουσεν ὁ ἰησοῦς ὅτι ἐξέβαλον αὐτὸν ἔξω καὶ εὑρὼν αὐτὸν εἶπεν αὐτῷ σὺ πιστεύεις εἰς τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and when he had found him, he said unto him, Dost thou believe on the Son of God?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit ille, et dixit: Quis est, Domine, ut credam in eum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ἐκεῖνος καὶ εἶπεν τίς ἐστιν κύριε ἵνα πιστεύσω εἰς αὐτόν
verses.king_james: He answered and said, Who is he, Lord, that I might believe on him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit ei Jesus: Et vidisti eum, et qui loquitur tecum, ipse est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ ἑώρακας αὐτὸν καὶ ὁ λαλῶν μετὰ σοῦ ἐκεῖνός ἐστιν
verses.king_james: And Jesus said unto him, Thou hast both seen him, and it is he that talketh with thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille ait: Credo, Domine. Et procidens adoravit eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἔφη πιστεύω κύριε καὶ προσεκύνησεν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And he said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit Jesus: In judicium ego in hunc mundum veni: ut qui non vident videant, et qui vident cæci fiant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν ὁ ἰησοῦς εἰς κρίμα ἐγὼ εἰς τὸν κόσμον τοῦτον ἦλθον ἵνα οἱ μὴ βλέποντες βλέπωσιν καὶ οἱ βλέποντες τυφλοὶ γένωνται
verses.king_james: And Jesus said, For judgment I am come into this world, that they which see not might see; and that they which see might be made blind.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et audierunt quidam ex pharisæis qui cum ipso erant, et dixerunt ei: Numquid et nos cæci sumus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἤκουσαν ἐκ τῶν φαρισαίων ταῦτα οἱ ὄντες μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ καὶ εἶπον αὐτῷ μὴ καὶ ἡμεῖς τυφλοί ἐσμεν
verses.king_james: And *some* of the Pharisees which were with him heard these words, and said unto him, Are we blind also?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit eis Jesus: Si cæci essetis, non haberetis peccatum. Nunc vero dicitis, Quia videmus: peccatum vestrum manet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς εἰ τυφλοὶ ἦτε οὐκ ἂν εἴχετε ἁμαρτίαν νῦν δὲ λέγετε ὅτι βλέπομεν ἡ οὖν ἁμαρτία ὑμῶν μένει
verses.king_james: Jesus said unto them, If ye were blind, ye should have no sin: but now ye say, We see; therefore your sin remaineth.
